page: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is a Cloud GPU instance configured with an Intel x86_64 processor. The system provides 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with a single thread per core, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 40.0 GB of system memory, yielding 5.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance features a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, representing a 0.3333 slice of the physical graphics processor, which delivers 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of a 740 GB NVMe SSD. Additionally, the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The fractional GPU allocation and shared CPU architecture offer a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring hardware acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. This resource density makes the instance suitable for mid-range deep learning inference, model prototyping, graphics rendering, and data processing tasks that benefit from GPU acceleration and fast local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is a Cloud GPU instance featuring an Intel x86_64 architecture with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores on a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 40.0 GB of RAM, providing 5.0 GB per core, alongside a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU that delivers 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Storage is supported by a bundled 740 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. By utilizing a fractional GPU and shared CPU resources, this configuration offers a cost-effective option for accelerated workloads. It is designed for tasks such as machine learning inference, small-scale model training, and parallel data processing pipelines.
